name: codebase-reorganization-template
description: Reusable template for systematic codebase reorganization. Define file movements, require() updates, and validation. Swarm executes deterministically with self-healing.

template_instructions.how_to_use: Copy this template and replace PLACEHOLDER sections with specific file paths and directory mappings. Pass to worker-bee swarm to execute.
benefits: Systematic require() path updates across codebase

operations.name: move-and-organize
operations.description: Move files from source to target directories, organized by concern

operations.name: update-requires-systematically
operations.description: Walk entire codebase and update require() calls to point to new file locations
process: For each moved file, find all files that require() it via grep
Calculate relative path from requiring file to new location
Update require() statement with new path
Log each update: [file] -> [old require] to [new require]
2. Check if file was moved to different location than expected
4. Update caller to use re-export path

operations.name: verify-structure
operations.description: Validate that reorganization matches intended structure
checks: All source files moved from source directories
No orphaned require() statements pointing to old locations

operations.name: validate-functionality
checks: Entry points in bin/ or cli/ can load without MODULE_NOT_FOUND

operations.name: validate-semantics
operations.description: Verify reorganization maintains code governance and coherence
checks: File anchors still reflect single responsibility

on_module_not_found: Attempt to locate file in alternative expected locations
If found elsewhere, update require() path to correct location
If not found, create re-export stub in original location pointing to new location
on_path_mismatch: Identify what path was expected vs. actual
Update require() to correct actual path
Log the mismatch and fix for review
on_require_cycle: Detect circular dependencies introduced by reorganization
Report the cycle for human review (don't auto-fix)
Suggest: move responsibility to third module or use dependency injection
self_healing_strategy.recovery_strategy: If unrecoverable after 3 retries, trigger rollback

rollback_plan.on_failure: Revert all file moves and require() updates to restore original state
rollback_plan.on_coherence_regression: If coherence drops >2%, automatically rollback and report
rollback_plan.on_test_failure: If tests fail after reorganization, rollback unless explicitly overridden

execution_guidelines.batch_approach: Execute in logical batches (concern-based). Validate after each batch before proceeding.
execution_guidelines.parallelization: Batches can run in parallel if they don't share file dependencies.
execution_guidelines.logging: Log every action: moves, require() updates, self-healing attempts, validation results.
execution_guidelines.transparency: Produce detailed report of all changes, decisions, and self-healing actions.
execution_guidelines.safety: When in doubt, stop and report rather than guess. Coherence and governance trump speed.
